title: [proposal] ability to change text overflow on the TextField
body:
If the user inputs more text than can fit into the available space of a single line `TextFormField`, then end of the text is simply cropped. 
In debug mode, an overflow warning is also displayed on screen.

Unlike the `Text` widget however, the `TextFormField` does not have an `overflow` parameter so that we can for example show an ellipsis in this situation.

Implementing `overflow` handling for the `TextFormField` will allow us to give valuable visual feedback to users.
comment_summary: Comments are largely me-too and bumps spanning multiple years with no root-cause analysis. One substantive comment links a related DevTools bug and notes @LongCatIsLooong is working on overflow ellipsis. The auto_size_text_field package is offered as an imperfect workaround; a TextPainter-based manual ellipsis workaround is also shared. No PR opened.

Hi there, this workaround helped me a lot:

```
Size measureTextSize(
      String text,
      TextStyle style, {
      int maxLines = 1,
      double maxWidth = double.infinity,
    }) {
      final TextPainter textPainter = TextPainter(
        text: TextSpan(text: text, style: style),
        textDirection: TextDirection.ltr,
        maxLines: maxLines,
      )..layout(minWidth: 0, maxWidth: maxWidth);

      return textPainter.size;
    }
```

```

...
```

title: Need to find how much of a long word could fit in one line before an unnatural line break
body:
As described in issue #35994, there are a number of use cases for doing non rectangular LTR or RTL text layout. Although not convenient, it is possible to do custom text layout by breaking a string into short runs and measuring each run as a `Paragraph` itself before painting it at some desired location. A word/line breaker would make this easier (see issue #50171), but for general cases breaking at a space works OK.

A problem arises, though, when there is text run that is longer than the available length for the line. Take the following example:

```
aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
```

There are no spaces to break at. That means that you have to measure every character or use some algorithm to test chunks of various lengths to see how much of it will fit. An unnatural break will be inserted at that location. The rest of the run will continue onto the next line.

A further complication is that some languages (like Mongolian and Arabic) use different size glyphs depending on the context. For example, here is a Mongolian word rendered correctly on the left, but the same word with the characters as they would look in isolation. 

![Mongolian word](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10300220/75090963-252da500-55a3-11ea-910b-dbd31511a16e.png)

The size is obviously different so simply taking the sum of the size of each character is not a viable option to find the length. Even taking a substring of the long word and measuring it would be different because the character at the break point would think it was at a final rather than a medial position and thus return the wrong size. A workaround would be to insert a Zero Width Joiner at the break point before measuring, but this is a lot of extra work compared to the feature request below. 

## Feature request

It would be very useful to have a feature like Android's `Paint.breakText` (see [example](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/45253574/does-paint-breaktext-include-the-character-at-the-maxwidth)). You feed it a string and a max length, and it tells you how many characters of that string will fit within the specified length.

title: [Web] [CanvasKit][Feature Request]: Load fonts as soon as detecting browser locale
body:
Currently, when I am testing the TexField I saw a weird effect. Below is the demo (web application).

![demo1](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/44708790/108788895-f4b48880-75b3-11eb-813d-c6a4db59e6bc.gif)

As you can see, initially when you typed in the Chinese character, it will show the gibberish character first then render the correct Chinese character. After the first try, it works kinda fine afterward. But sometimes you can still see the gibberish character interchangeably. However, I am not sure if this is the nature of how it is... but to me, something seems wrong here.

Here is another demo for Japanese!

![demo2_jp](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/44708790/108790134-ee73db80-75b6-11eb-94ea-045535c072d4.gif)


Based on @yjbanov commented in #76473, it might be flutter loading fonts dynamically. Flutter detects that it doesn't have enough fonts and that is likely the reason.

comment_summary: Comments identify the root cause as CanvasKit dynamically loading CJK fonts when unknown characters are first encountered; switching to the HTML renderer avoids the issue. The workaround of using a custom font family (bundled) also resolves it. Community notes this affects Hebrew and Arabic too, and persists through 3.32.4. The issue was marked P1 but has had no recent owner; status is stalled.

title: Mixing RTL and LTR text bugs
body:
We are seeing strange behavior when mixing RTL and LTR text.

Notably:

- Type arabic, Type chinese, Type arabic, switch to chinese keyboard, delete. The arabic will delete all at once.
- Emojis work strangely when mixing with RTL and LTR.
- Caret positioning is not consistent.

There are a few other minor issues that I am not clear on how to describe.
comment_summary: Early comment identified root cause: ParagraphStyle directionality is LTR for mixed text, causing spaces to be inserted at the wrong end of Arabic spans. The SkParagraph switch (#39420) was expected to fix many behaviors. Subsequent comments report partial improvements but deletion with opposite-direction keyboards remains broken.
comments_raw: After some investigation, when mixing LTR and RTL text, the ParagraphStyle's directionality is LTR. This is causing the space to be inserted at the wrong end of the arabic span. We should handle arabic spaces in a RTL manner even if the paragraph direction is LTR. The correct solution to this is to change the paragraph to RTL when dealing with RTL text, however, when arabic snippets are inline with primarily LTR text, this may not be possible.

Caret positioning has vastly improved with https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/9489

The primary remaining issue is handling deletion properly with keyboards of opposite direction.
This should be put on hold until https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/39420 (SkParagraph switch) is completed, as SkParagraph will fix many of these behaviors.

another problem is when we have rtl and ltr text mixed up like (english & persian) and the we put then `overflow` property to `TextOverflow.ellipsis`. the 3 dots will apear in the middle between the persian and english text instead of it apearing at the end
Any Updates?
this feels like an eternity :cry:

title: Trailing space doesn't work with TextField with TextAlign.right
body:
TextField  Widget Option textAlign Setting for [TextAlign.right] ,input  space key cursor fault


 `TextField(
            textAlign: TextAlign.right,
          )
`
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug persists through 2025 on all tested stable versions. The trailing space is visually trimmed/not rendered when TextAlign.right is used. A Skia bug was filed at bugs.chromium.org/p/skia/issues/detail?id=11933 and accepted. One commenter notes SkParagraph treats \r as a soft line break with CodeUnitFlag 0x0006, which may be related. A layout workaround using IntrinsicWidth inside a Row was suggested. No fix has landed in Flutter; status is stalled.

title: Arrow keys in RTL move the wrong way
comment_summary: Visual order (left = left, right = right) traversal support is acknowledged as a planned feature but not yet implemented. A commenter noted the existing Intent/Action system uses a `forward` boolean that may need to be extended to support left/right explicitly. Status: stalled, planned but not implemented.

title: Feature request: Setting paragraph distance in Text and TextField
body: Currently, we have no control over paragraph spacing in both Text and TextField. This feature is important in text stying.
comments_raw: any news update?

I would like to add information to this: Flutter currently has letterSpacing and even wordSpacing, I don't think paragraphSpacing would be hard. Instead of applying horizontal space to each " ", it would apply vertical space to each "\n".
Also spacing control for `u2029` and `u2028` will be great.
Any updates ? 🙂 we should be able to define this on a TextStyle like in Figma
This issue is missing a priority label. Please set a priority label when adding the `triaged-framework` label.

title: Selection of any justified-text is inaccurate in non-latin languages
body:
I hope I can clarify this issue because it is a little weird. It took me a while to figure out when exactly does it occur.

Here is a very primitive application:

```dart
// main.dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
void main() => runApp(MaterialApp(
  home: Scaffold(
    appBar: AppBar(),
    body: Padding(
      padding: const EdgeInsets.all(18.0),
      child: TextField(
        textAlign: TextAlign.justify,
        maxLines: null,
      ),
    ),
  ),
));
```

Now, type any latin charachter text. Try to select any single or multiple words, you will find that the selection blue highlight matches accurately the boundaries of the word(s). See this screen shot:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3346502/64191394-5a3e2200-ceb3-11e9-82a9-6f48d6333256.png)

_____________________

Then, put some Korean text. You will find that some words are poorly highlighted:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3346502/64191512-9bcecd00-ceb3-11e9-85ea-4002d85f54da.png)

> 한국어(韓國語)는 대한민국과 조선민주주의인민공화국의 공용어로, 대한민국에서는 한국어 또는 한국말이라고 부르고, 조선민주주의인민공화국에서는 조선말이라고 부른다.

_____________________

Also in Arabic texts (it does not matter here that the `textDirection` is not RTL. Both RTL and LTR have the same issue):

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3346502/64191648-e18b9580-ceb3-11e9-8e5c-6fe5a3bb1dba.png)

> العربية لغة رسمية في كل دول الوطن العربي إضافة إلى كونها لغة رسمية في تشاد وإريتريا وإسرائيل. وهي إحدى اللغات الرسمية الست في منظمة الأمم المتحدة، ويُحتفل باليوم العالمي للغة العربية في 18 ديسمبر كذكرى اعتماد العربية بين لغات العمل في الأمم المتحدة.

_____________________

Same in Hebrew:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3346502/64191829-5068ee80-ceb4-11e9-84f2-780841b134b3.png)

> עִבְרִית היא שפה שמית, ממשפחת השפות האפרו-אסיאתיות, הידועה כשפתם של היהודים ושל השומרונים, אשר ניב מודרני שלה (עברית ישראלית) הוא שפתה הרשמית של מדינת ישראל, מעמד שעוגן בשנת 2018 בחוק יסוד: ישראל – מדינת הלאום של העם היהודי.

_____________________

Just copy these texts and paste them in the input field, then try to select one word. In all cases above I just long tapped on one word and the highlight missed the word boundry as shown.

It does have to be a text field or input though. Any justified selectable text would reproduce the same behavior. I tried in both stable and master channels.

Here is my `flutter doctor` output:

```
Doctor summary (to see all details, run flutter doctor -v):
[✓] Flutter (Channel master, v1.9.8-pre.54, on Linux, locale en_US.UTF-8)
 
[✓] Android toolchain - develop for Android devices (Android SDK version 29.0.2)
[✓] Android Studio (version 3.5)
[✓] VS Code (version 1.37.1)
[✓] Connected device (1 available)

• No issues found!
```
comment_summary: Comments narrow the scope: the inaccurate selection highlight affects Korean, Arabic, Hebrew, and Persian scripts but not Cyrillic (Russian). Using a language-appropriate font (e.g., Amiri for Arabic) fixes the issue for pure RTL text, but mixed Latin+RTL text remains broken even with a proper font. The `selectionHeightStyle`/`selectionWidthStyle` options partially helped but did not fully resolve the issue. The problem is present in all text-alignment modes for RTL scripts, not only justified text. Status: open since 2019, no PR, long-standing stall.
comments_raw: CC @GaryQian

It does not have to be a text input to reproduce the issue. Any selectable text would show the same behavior (text inputs, SelectableText, SelectableText.rich ... etc). So I think it's better to remove `a: text input` label.

@OsamaAbbas The labels are not exclusive, if it impacts an area or is reasonably relevant, we should add the label so that the right people can find the right bug.

This looks like we are somehow missing the justification_x_offset from the code_units in LibTxt. The curious part is somehow, alphabetic text works, which indicates it isn't a blatant omission of the justification_x_offset but rather something more subtle.
Russian language does NOT have this issue. (I thought it would, because it uses non latin chars)

A more likely explanation is that some scripts treat spaces as forming a new run of text, while latin script treats spaces as a part of the same run of text. I'm reviewing the justify logic to see how we deal with spaces as a delimiter for runs and if we are justifying properly there.

I really appreciate your effort. Thank you.

Maybe related: Mixed text (latin and non latin characters) breaks the RTL justification but fixes the selection accuracy! However, not all combinations have this effect, some combinations only fixes the selection accuracy on the same line. I did many trials but have not recorded them. By a 'combination' I mean: single latin character, one character plus a space, one word without spaces, multi-words ... etc.

The handles of the selection highlight (in non latin text) is also inaccurate in the other `textAlign` cases in the case of multi word selection.

Third problem: the spaces between words are given a smaller height than the words.

![20190909_151634](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3346502/64507214-0ef19c80-d315-11e9-9554-7b398dc5745f.jpg)

An important update: changing the font to an appropriate Arabic font (I used Amiri Font) solves the accuracy problem. It also makes spaces have the same height as the surrounding text.

I will try with a Korean font later.

You can now use `selectionHeightStyle` and `selectionWidthStyle` to choose how the selection highlighting will look. See https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/48917

I recommend trying `max` or `strut`
Actually, the issue above only partially resolves this. Reopening!

Any updates on this issue? [This](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/39755#issuecomment-529315991) is something we're facing too in Arabic text selection no matter what the text alignment is. It's not just a justification issue, and I think the title should reflect that. We've tried different fonts and no solution. Even on latest release v1.17.1. Thank you.

incorrect selection in  arabic (RTL) language

### Update: After investigating a bit more into this issue I can confirm that it has to do something with the font being used. In another application with a custom font containing glyphs for Persian language it's working as intended but using the default font brings back the issue. ![Nov-01-2021 13-35-49](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8766492/139655432-5a48c810-52d9-446e-b5a1-70a31d3295a2.gif) Top textfield uses a custom font and bottom textfield is using default font (I guess Roboto because of `MaterialApp`). --- 2021 is about to end and it's been open since 2019. Seems like Flutter's non-English community is so small since it only got 6 thumbs up in more than 2 years and has been inactive in a year! Can any of Flutter's contributors define a clear path on possible solutions to fixing this issue? I'm willing to start working on it but I'm not familiar with Flutter's huge codebase and this issue seems to be related to many places from Flutter's engine to the Flutter's text related widgets. title: TextFormField with textAlign: TextAlign.right  whitespace doesn't show unless text is entered.
body:
I use TextFormField and property textAlign: TextAlign.right in my web application but I see prob when typing space, it don't show if don't have any text after it :(
 Please help!
comment_summary: Comments confirm the trailing whitespace rendering bug with `TextAlign.right` is reproducible on all platforms (web, macOS, Android, Windows) on stable and master. The space exists in the string but is not rendered because of text alignment trimming. A creative hack workaround (transparent TextField overlaid with a Text widget) is provided but is not suitable for production. The issue is missing a priority label as noted by a bot. Status: open, stalled, workaround available.

title: TextFormField (and TextField) widgets do not wrap text correctly
body: Similar to the issues reported in #18761, the input fields in Flutter suffer from the same disfunction. Currently, the text input fields in Flutter do not wrap their input correctly. Instead of breaking a word in the middle and wrapping the remainder to the next line, Flutter is breaking the string at the word boundary closest to the end of the line, which is non buono. #### Flutter #### Chrome Web browsers correctly split the string at the character that would otherwise overflow. #### Code snippet ```dart TextFormField( decoration: InputDecoration(border: OutlineInputBorder()), minLines: null, maxLines: null, initialValue: 'https://www.example.com/some/thingweirdisgoingon.here', ) ```
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug is distinct from #18761 (which is about read-only Text widgets), and the issue persists through at least Flutter 3.10.6 / Dart 3.0 on macOS. Multiple commenters reproduced it with long URLs in `TextFormField` using `FractionallySizedBox`. No proposed fix or PR exists; status is open and unresolved.
comments_raw: Have you ever found a solution to this problem @benPesso?
@Sembauke Not really, no. I haven't tested it in a while (after #18761 was resolved). If you're experiencing this, I guess that fix did not resolve this issue.
@benPesso This seems like it it fixed in the newest version of Flutter. But please confirm yourself.

@Sembauke  Still not really working as expected.
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/921173/07a721d1-319c-4ec9-8aaa-39aef3ec6b8c

```

  widthFactor: .8,

    decoration: const InputDecoration(border: OutlineInputBorder()),

),
```

title: [Proposal] Add support for automatically switching text input to `RTL` or `LTR` based on first character typed
body:
<!-- Thank you for using Flutter!

     If you are looking for support, please check out our documentation
     or consider asking a question on Stack Overflow:
      * https://flutter.dev/
      * https://api.flutter.dev/
      * https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/flutter?sort=frequent

     If you have found a bug or if our documentation doesn't have an answer
     to what you're looking for, then fill out the template below. Please read
     our guide to filing a bug first: https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports
-->

## Use case

<!--
     Please tell us the problem you are running into that led to you wanting
     a new feature.

     Is your feature request related to a problem? Please give a clear and
     concise description of what the problem is.

     Describe the alternative solutions you've considered. Is there a package
     on pub.dev/flutter that already solves this?
-->

In multilingual text fields, Bidirectional text input is automatically recognised. For example, typing `ب ` as the first character in `Whatsapp` places the cursor the right side and typing `b` as the first, places the cursor at the left side. That is it automatically detects the language and makes the text input appropriate, whether it is an `RTL` or `LTR` language.

## Proposal

<!--
     Briefly but precisely describe what you would like Flutter to be able to do.

     Consider attaching something showing what you are imagining:
      * images
      * videos
      * code samples

     Does this have to be provided by Flutter directly, or can it be provided
     by a package on pub.dev/flutter? If so, maybe consider implementing and
     publishing such a package rather than filing a bug.
-->

I think flutter must detect bidirectional text input out of the box, no matter what is the current `Directionality.of(context)`. The same behaviour stated above should be exhibited in flutter textfields.
comment_summary: Discussion notes the Bidi class exists in Flutter's intl. Suggestion to add an auto value similar to HTML's dir attribute. Noted as technically feasible via ICU ubidi API. Adding to TextPainter suggested, along with needing a low-level API for WidgetSpan writing directions.
comments_raw:
Not only `TextField`, but also `Text` must detect bidirectional text out of the box, I think having a `bool` property like `matchContentDirection` with default value as `true` is awesome, I'm not sure may be `TextAlign.auto` as default text direction is better
 Detecting bidirectional text must be added to `TextField` itself and all text in it like `hint` , `helper`, `error`

> In multilingual text input, usually it detect Bidirectional text

Hi @sm2017, I'm not particularly familiar with `Bidirectional` text input, can you expand more on this?

See https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bidirectional_text

There is a bidi class in flutter https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/intl/Bidi-class.html

It is impossible to implement my feature request in application layer, because we have dependencies that has text field or text widget

@sm2017, my question was poorly phrased, what I meant was can you expand a bit more on this statement you made earlier?

> In multilingual text input, usually it detect Bidirectional text

Let me describe default behavior of text input in Android os, Chrome, Whatsapp and ...

In your Chrome search bar, try to type `ب` , feel free to copy and paste it, you can see the direction of text is detected as LRT, AFAIK it's a common behavior in most operation systems and applications

It's Bidirectional text input as it's context-aware

@sm2017, thanks for the insight. 
I have updated the proposal to make it clearer.

It might be a good idea to add a new value called `auto` similar to the web.

For reference here are the docs on `dir`.
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Global_attributes/dir
I need this, it's so useful. I added some code to `onChange` property of `TextField`s in my app to set text direction based on content.

@goderbauer This seems technically feasible (https://unicode-org.github.io/icu-docs/apidoc/dev/icu4c/ubidi_8h.html#a165ae276878c7a54fd8b067db0d12f40). 

Does it make sense to add this to `TextPainter`? I guess for `WidgetSpan`s we'll need a new low-level API that gives us the real writing direction of a paragraph, before we can build those inline widgets with the correct writing directions?

title: `dart:ui.LineMetrics` should include the line boundaries
body:
The information is currently manually stripped when constructing a `LineMetrics`:
https://github.com/google/skia/blob/a92b268f7d78e0069b2a61b22cb7ce615d12a60e/modules/skparagraph/include/Metrics.h#L39-L93

It will be much easier to implement vertical caret movements for up/down arrow keys with the line boundary information included. This corresponds to a todo item in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/90684 .
comment_summary: A commenter confirmed the need for TextRange in LineMetrics and described the current workaround: calling Paragraph.getPositionForOffset to derive line text ranges, which is inconvenient and indirect.
comments_raw:
Up vote for this issue. It would be very convenient if the `LineMetrics` includes `TextRange` for the current line. 
Right now, our approach of getting the text range is by calling the `getPositionForOffset` method that `Paragraph` provides.

title: TextField/TextFormField labelText and hintText should be right-aligned with TextDirection.rtl
body:
There is forgotten attribute **write** for the `labelText of(TextFormField or TextField)` from right to left.
I tried with `textAlign`, `textDirection`. Although, it works with `hintText` and the user input, it doesn't work with the `labelText`.


![Screenshot](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15935347/65644781-86058100-dff5-11e9-82f0-60321192c474.png)
[textfield.txt](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/files/3655101/textfield.txt)



```dart
TextFormField(
        textAlign: Language.lang  == 'NotArabic'  ?TextAlign.left:TextAlign.right,
        validator: (value) {
          return Validators().validateEmail(value);
        },
        controller: _emailController,
        decoration: new InputDecoration(
            hintStyle: new TextStyle(color: Colors.grey[500],),
            labelStyle: new TextStyle(color: Colors.grey[900],),
            hintText: 'hint Email',
            labelText: 'label Email',
            fillColor: Colors.white70),
        textDirection:
            Language.lang == 'NotArabic' ? TextDirection.ltr : TextDirection.rtl,
      ) 
```

title: Incorrect selection area in RTL TextField.
body:
A dot at the end of a line that is followed by another line makes the text selection area incorrect!
First, create a simple app with an RTL multiline TextField. (or use my sample code.)

Steps to Reproduce:
1. Add a line with this text: "Select a word and see the selected area."
2. Add another line
3. Double click on the "word" to select it.

Expected:
The selection area should match the "word" area.

Actual:
The selected area is not matched with the "word" area.

<details>
<summary>Sample code</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() =>
  runApp(MaterialApp(
    home: Scaffold(
      body: Column(
        mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center,
        children: [
          TextField(
            controller: TextEditingController(text: '\nSelect a word and see the incorrect selected area.\n'),
            textDirection: TextDirection.rtl,
            maxLines: null,
          ),
          TextField(
            controller: TextEditingController(text: '\nSelect a word and see the correct selected area.\n'),
            maxLines: null,
          ),
        ],
      ),
    ),
  ));
```
</details>
comment_summary: Triage confirmed reproduction on master 3.7 on Android and iOS. Root cause identified: Skia SkParagraph's `getRectsForRange` API; a Skia bug was filed at https://bugs.chromium.org/p/skia/issues/detail?id=14035. A whitespace workaround was suggested.
comments_raw:
## Triage report

I can reproduce this issue on `Master (3.7.0-9.0.pre.11)`. The selection as an offset when selecting a word. On pressing select all, the handles are both at the end of the text, instead of on opposite ends. Reproducible on both Android and iOS

<details>
<summary>Code Sample (Same as OP)</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() => runApp(
      MaterialApp(

              TextField(
                controller: TextEditingController(
                    text:
                        '\nSelect a word and see the incorrect selected area.\n'),
                textDirection: TextDirection.rtl,
                maxLines: null,
              ),
              TextField(
                controller: TextEditingController(
                    text:
                        '\nSelect a word and see the correct selected area.\n'),
                maxLines: null,
              ),
            ],
          ),
        ),
      ),
    );
```

</details>

##### Selecting a word

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/50555895/207884621-0682bbb2-be55-4eae-872e-8a9c8d60bf97.png)

##### Selecting all

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/50555895/207884900-4ffd904d-efc9-4400-bad4-e4b0f0795407.png)
just add white space between /n and words

```
Scaffold(
      body: Column(
        mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center,
        children: [
          TextField(
            controller: TextEditingController(text: '\n Select a word and see the incorrect selected area. \n'),
            textDirection: TextDirection.rtl,
            maxLines: null,
          ),
          TextField(
            controller: TextEditingController(text: '\nSelect a word and see the correct selected area.\n'),
            maxLines: null,
          ),
        ],
      ),
    )
```
This is caused by the implementation of the `getRectsForRange` API in the Skia SkParagraph text layout library.

Filed a Skia bug: https://bugs.chromium.org/p/skia/issues/detail?id=14035

title: RTL TextField breaks when inserting emojis between existing emojis
body:
### Steps to reproduce

When using a TextField with RTL text direction, inserting an emoji between two existing emojis sometimes breaks the text, showing ? character.

### Expected results

Inserting emojis between existing emojis in an RTL TextField should render correctly.

### Actual results

The inserted emoji breaks the text and may render as ?.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
TextField(textDirection: TextDirection.rtl)
```

</details>


### Screenshots or Video

<details open>
<summary>Screenshots / Video demonstration</summary>

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/706052f5-2309-4bdf-a44b-6ab05848f8a7

</details>


### Logs

<details colose><summary>Logs</summary>

```console
n/a
```

</details>


### Flutter Doctor output

<details colose><summary>Doctor output</summary>

```console
n/a
```

</details>
comment_summary: Comments identify the root cause: on macOS and Linux, dynamically changing `textDirection` during IME composition triggers a `wstring_convert::to_bytes` exception in the engine (built with `-fno-exceptions`), causing an abort; on Android the IME composing state is invalidated, producing `?`/replacement characters. iOS and web cannot reproduce. A crash log from macOS shows the engine decoding an invalid JSON surrogate pair. A comment in #183112 indicates this cannot be fixed in the framework and requires fixes in each affected embedder (macOS, Linux). Status: needs engine/embedder fix.
comments_raw:
The issue behaves differently across platforms.

On Flutter Desktop (Windows/Linux/macOS), dynamically changing textDirection on an active TextField can crash the Flutter Engine. During the rebuild of the native text input layout, the engine attempts to convert a wide string (`std::wstring`) to UTF-8 using `wstring_convert::to_bytes`.
If the text is in an intermediate or invalid Unicode state (for example, during IME composition or layout recalculation), this conversion fails and throws a std::range_error with the message:
```
wstring_convert: to_bytes error
```


Since the Flutter Engine is built with -fno-exceptions, the exception cannot be caught, resulting in an application abort.

On mobile platforms (Android/iOS), the app usually does not crash because a different text input pipeline is used. However, changing textDirection during active text composition may invalidate the IME composing state, causing unsupported or incomplete Unicode sequences to be replaced with a fallback character (**displayed as ? or �**).
@puneetkukreja98 this is a problem in macos as well.

@warioddly I’m able to reproduce this issue on macOS as well. Thanks for reporting it.
### LOGS

```
Lost connection to device.

```

Other platforms: * ✅ iOS - could not repro * ✅ Web - could not repro * ✅ Windows - could not repro * ❌ macOS - app crashes * ❌ Linux - Text field shows `?` instead of emoji

title: [web] Support line height + word spacing in text fields
body:
TextInput.setStyle method call informs the engine about the styling info of an editable text.

This method does not support line height and word spacing.

title: TextField RTL input problem with LTR letters/numbers while obscureText is true
body:
I believe there's a bug with textfield when writing LTR characters in an obscured field.

The characters are not written in the correct direction while typing (this issue appears only when obscureText is true).

The input value actually is submitted correctly with no problems and also if the password is made visible it is shown in the right way with no issues, the issue only is while typing so it makes it feel like if the password is written in a reversed direction.

**While typing the letters are written to the left not to the right as expected.**

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Create an empty application.
2. Add a TextField  or TextFormField to the application.
3. Set textDirection: TextDirection.rtl
4. Set obscureText: true.
5. type something in English.


![screen-20201127-184354](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/30502213/100470646-51052a80-30e1-11eb-84a7-838968ba2232.gif)


### Expected behavior

To act the same like when obscureText: false.

### Flutter Doctor

[✓] Flutter (Channel stable, 1.22.4, on Mac OS X 10.15.7 19H2 darwin-x64, locale en-EG)
 
[✓] Android toolchain - develop for Android devices (Android SDK version 30.0.0)
 
[✓] Xcode - develop for iOS and macOS (Xcode 11.6)
[✓] Android Studio (version 4.0)
[✓] VS Code (version 1.51.1)
[✓] Connected device (1 available)

title: Share code between RenderParagraph and RenderEditable
body:
In https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/83537, we realized that a lot of code is shared between RenderParagraph and RenderEditable, especially for WidgetSpan support.

As we consider adding WidgetSpan support for SelectableText as well (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/38474), this leads us to prefer code reuse rather than three extremely similar codebases to maintain.

We may be able to restructure it to inherit, or potentially a mixin, though I am still unclear how sharable the code actually is.

title: TextField doesn't handle trailing space as characters if textAlign is TextAlign.right/TextAlign.center
body:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Run `flutter create bug`.
2. Update main.dart as follows: ... 
```import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'TextField trailing space',
      theme: ThemeData(
        primarySwatch: Colors.blue,
      ),
      home: MyHomePage(title: 'Flutter Demo Home Page'),
    );
  }
}

class MyHomePage extends StatefulWidget {
  MyHomePage({Key? key, required this.title}) : super(key: key);
  final String title;

  @override
  _MyHomePageState createState() => _MyHomePageState();
}

class _MyHomePageState extends State<MyHomePage> {
  var textAlign = TextAlign.center;

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      appBar: AppBar(
        title: Text(widget.title),
      ),
      body: Center(
        child: Column(
          mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center,
          children: <Widget>[
            Text(
              'You have pushed the button this many times:',
            ),
            TextField(
              maxLines: null,
              textAlign: textAlign,
            ),
          ],
        ),
      ),
      floatingActionButton: FloatingActionButton(
        onPressed: () {setState(() {
          textAlign = (textAlign == TextAlign.right) ? TextAlign.center : TextAlign.right;
        });},
        tooltip: 'Increment',
        child: Icon(Icons.add),
      ), 
    );
  }
}
```

3. Write a single character, then press space repeatedly until the end of line is reached. Then type a character eg 'a'.

**Expected results:** proper line break for space characters/ space characters push back input string textAlign is TextAlign.right

**Actual results:** space characters are stopped at the end of TextField and won't be handle as normal character input (break into new line, pushing the input string if textAlign is TextAlign.right).

comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible on stable and master by a triager (video provided); trailing spaces do not push text or break lines as expected when `textAlign` is `TextAlign.center` or `TextAlign.right`. An initial comment wrongly suggested the behavior was correct; subsequent comments and a video confirmed it is not. A bot comment noted a missing priority label. No root cause was identified in the comments and no fix or PR was proposed; issue is stalled.

title: letterSpacing in TextField with monospace font is only applied to right side of the first character until a second character is entered, then letterSpacing is applied correctly to both sides
body:
<!-- Thank you for using Flutter!

     If you are looking for support, please check out our documentation
     or consider asking a question on Stack Overflow:
      * https://flutter.dev/
      * https://api.flutter.dev/
      * https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/flutter?sort=frequent

     If you have found a bug or if our documentation doesn't have an answer
     to what you're looking for, then fill out the template below. Please read
     our guide to filing a bug first: https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports
-->

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- Please include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. Run the sample app here: https://github.com/acoutts/flutter_text_bug/blob/main/lib/main.dart
2. Enter some characters in the text field

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->
The letter spacing should be applied to all characters equally no matter the length of the input text.


**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->
When only one character is entered, the letterSpacing is only added to the right of the character.
When a second character is entered, then the letter spacing is correctly applied to both sides of the first character.

Video demonstration:
https://giphy.com/gifs/blxgQ70RIoVvJybyW0

While the video demonstrates the behavior on iOS, I tested on an Android device and confirmed the same behavior there.

<details>
<summary>Code sample</summary>

<!--
      Please create a minimal reproducible sample that shows the problem
      and attach it below between the lines with the backticks.

      To create it you can use `flutter create bug` command and update the `main.dart` file.

      Alternatively, you can use https://dartpad.dev/
      which is capable of creating and running small Flutter apps.

      Without this we will unlikely be able to progress on the issue, and because of that
      we regretfully will have to close it.
-->

```dart
           TextField(
              style: TextStyle(
                fontFamily: 'NotoMono-Regular',
                fontSize: MediaQuery.of(context).size.width * 0.086,
                letterSpacing: MediaQuery.of(context).size.width * 0.082,
              ),
            )
```

</details>

<details>
  <summary>Logs</summary>

<!--

      log output below between the lines with the backticks. If there is an

      to explain how to solve the issue.
-->

```
```

<!--


comment_summary: Comments confirmed the bug reproduces on both stable and master across Android and iOS. A fix was submitted to Skia (https://skia-review.googlesource.com/c/skia/+/541978) and subsequently merged. One commenter reported the issue was still present in Flutter 3.3 beta but then confirmed it was fixed, leaving the issue open pending stable promotion.

What is supposed to be the behavior here? Is Flutters behavior different from native iOS, Android, Mac, or Chrome?

It seems to be a regression as it didn’t happen in past versions of flutter. 

The expected behavior is if I specify letter spacing, it should be applied equally to all characters no matter the input length.

As demonstrated above, the letter spacing is initially only applied to the right side of the first character if the input length is 1. Only once a second character is added does the letter spacing then get added to the left side of the first character.
Submitted a fix to Skia: https://skia-review.googlesource.com/c/skia/+/541978
I see that one is merged now - when can we expect it to be available in flutter?
Sadly it’s still happening even in the latest beta (3.3).

Correction - it is now fixed! Great news 🙌 
Should we leave this open until it makes it to stable?

title: No good way to get line metrics for `Text`/`TextField` based widgets.
comment_summary: Root cause identified: the user's `TextPainter` does not use the same `TextStyle` as the `TextField` (which applies material theme styles), causing layout width mismatches. A team member (@dnfield) initially proposed using `controller.buildTextSpan(context: context, withComposing: false)` as a fix but later confirmed it does not reliably carry the correct styles. The confirmed workaround is to manually replicate the exact `TextStyle` the `TextField` applies (e.g., resolving from the material theme). @LongCatIsLooong mentioned a planned framework-level API exposing immutable text layout metrics via a callback (e.g., `onTextLayoutChanged`) but noted a `TextEditingController` cannot be the right place since one controller can serve multiple fields with different styles. Currently stalled with no PR.

> ```dart
>     final textPainter = TextPainter(
>       text: TextSpan(text: text),
>       textDirection: TextDirection.ltr,
>       maxLines: null,
>     );
> 
>     textPainter.layout(maxWidth: MediaQuery.of(context).size.width);
> ```
> 
> is probably not a reliable way to measure the text layout of a `TextField`. `TextField`s apply additional styles (from themes for example since it's a material widget) that may change the text layout drastically. For example the above text painter will use a font size of 14 by default but the material default is probably larger (and iirc it even depends on the locale).

Even if you manually set the text size to 14 and specify a static font we have issues. The paragraph width and the line width are not matching the actual painting width, so the line metrics are coming out wrong.
I could have sworn I was getting different results on this before and now it's back to misbehaving. Reopening while I look at it again.
Argh, this method is not actually getting the style from the text the way I thought it was.

Ok.

The framework does not appear to have a way to tell you the `TextStyle` used in a `TextField`.

I think it would be ideal if the `TextEditingController` had some way to access this. Perhaps some method parallel to `buildTextSpan` that did not take a `TextStyle` argument but instead used the text style of the widget it's controlling.

/cc @justinmc @LongCatIsLooong who probably have good ideas about this.
(My original confusion probably related to prints I added to the framework that looked right - I can confirm that if you use the same exact text style in both places things work out).
The `triaged-engine` label is irrelevant if there is no `team-engine` label or `fyi-engine` label.

I'm currently trying to expose more text layout metrics, the end goal is to expose an immutable interface for querying the current text layout, and allowing developers to listen for text layout changes. I'm planning to add that to the text related render objects but since there doesn't seem to be an easy way to get the render objects from `Text` or `EditableText` I guess adding a callback to these widgets would be the easiest way to expose them? E.g., `Text.onTextLayoutChanged(TextLayout newLayout)`, `EditableText.onTextLayoutChanged(TextLayout newLayout, Offset newOffset)`. But the catch is that it's not allowed to call `setState` synchronously in these callbacks.

A `TextEditingController` can be assigned to multiple text fields that use different paragraph styles and have completely different text layout so it's probably not the best place to do that.

> A `TextEditingController` can be assigned to multiple text fields that use different paragraph styles and have completely different text layout so it's probably not the best place to do that.

What happens to the value of a TextEditingController in this case?

Or is it that `TextEditingValue`s can have multiple styles associated with them?

2 `TextField`s can share the same `TextEditingController`. These 2 `TextField`s will share the same editing states (text, selection, composing region), but the `TextStyle`s and `ParagraphStyle` used to render the text can be different since the `Theme` and the widget configurations they apply may be different.

I can get the lines from the `Text` widget by following [this](https://stackoverflow.com/a/78341140/17798537). But this solution does not work for `TextField`. I think it will work if we know the default `style` of the `TextField` when we set it to null. At this moment we need to use the same style to make it work.
It will be better if the `Text` and `TextField` widgets have the `.onTextLineChange` function.
@dnfield is that any helpful? https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/151952#issuecomment-2306329585

title: App highlights / selects trailing whitespaces in a multi-line textfield.
comment_summary: Root cause not identified. Confirmed on Android, iOS, and desktop when maxLines or minLines is set; does not occur on web. Native Android and iOS apps do not highlight trailing whitespace. Routed to @LongCatIsLooong.
comments_raw: I was able to replicate the reported behavior. The issue is, for multiline textfield, selecting the text in first line also highlights the trailing whitespace in accordance to next line's length, as shown below: While if we try to select the next line's text if length of it is less than first line, it doesn't highlight the trailing whitespace: Above behavior occurs Android, iOS and desktop if we pass a value to `maxLines` or also adding `minLines`, but not in web, wherein, it doesn't highlight the trailing whitespace of first line: I checked the native behavior on Android and iOS using Samsung / Notes app and there, it doesn't highlight the whitespace: I tried to find if this is an existing issue filed already but couldn't find an obvious / similar issue or if I missed to find it if already present. Based on my findings above, keeping the issue open for team's tracking. ``` stable: 3.35.2 master : 3.36.0-1.0.pre-305 ```
cc @LongCatIsLooong

title: Vertical baseline alignment mismatch between Text and collapsed TextField when changing TextLeadingDistribution
body:
### Steps to reproduce Similar (but different) to https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/144502 Add a row, with a text and textfield with the same font style, the textfield decoration should be `InputDecoration.collapsed()` ### Expected results The text and textfield should align vertically. The base lines should align. ### Actual results Depending on the leading distribution the TextField will either go high or go low. ### Code sample
Code sample ```dart import 'package:flutter/material.dart'; void main() { runApp(const MainApp()); } class MainApp extends StatelessWidget { const MainApp({super.key}); @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { const textStyleEven = TextStyle( inherit: false, fontSize: 180, height: 1, letterSpacing: 0, textBaseline: TextBaseline.alphabetic, leadingDistribution: TextLeadingDistribution.even, color: Colors.black, ); final textStyleProp = textStyleEven.copyWith(leadingDistribution: TextLeadingDistribution.proportional); final text = 'X'; final width = 120.0; return MaterialApp( home: Scaffold( body: Column( mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center, children: [ // EVEN leading distribution Row( children: [ SizedBox( width: width, child: Text(text, style: textStyleEven), ), // this one is fine SizedBox( width: width, child: TextField( controller: TextEditingController(text: text), decoration: InputDecoration( hintText: '', contentPadding: EdgeInsets.zero, border: InputBorder.none, ), style: textStyleEven, strutStyle: StrutStyle(fontSize: textStyleEven.fontSize, height: textStyleEven.height), textAlignVertical: TextAlignVertical.center, ), ), // goes low SizedBox( width: width, child: TextField( controller: TextEditingController(text: text), decoration: InputDecoration.collapsed(hintText: ''), style: textStyleEven, strutStyle: StrutStyle(fontSize: textStyleEven.fontSize, height: textStyleEven.height), textAlignVertical: TextAlignVertical.center, ), ), ], ), SizedBox(height: 100), // PROPORTIONAL leading distribution Row( children: [ SizedBox( width: width, child: Text(text, style: textStyleProp), ), // Goes very high SizedBox( width: width, child: TextField( controller: TextEditingController(text: text), decoration: InputDecoration( hintText: '', contentPadding: EdgeInsets.zero, border: InputBorder.none, ), style: textStyleProp, strutStyle: StrutStyle(fontSize: textStyleProp.fontSize, height: textStyleProp.height), textAlignVertical: TextAlignVertical.center, ), ), // goes a little high SizedBox( width: width, child: TextField( controller: TextEditingController(text: text), decoration: InputDecoration.collapsed(hintText: ''), style: textStyleProp, strutStyle: StrutStyle(fontSize: textStyleProp.fontSize, height: textStyleProp.height), textAlignVertical: TextAlignVertical.center, ), ), ], ), ], ), ), ); } } ```
### Screenshots or Video
Screenshots / Video demonstration
### Logs
Logs ```console [Paste your logs here] ```

Doctor output ```console [√] Flutter (Channel stable, 3.41.6, on Microsoft Windows [Version 10.0.26200.8037], locale en-GB) [501ms] • Flutter version 3.41.6 on channel stable at C:\sdks\flutter • Upstream repository https://github.com/flutter/flutter.git • Framework revision db50e20168 (34 hours ago), 2026-03-25 16:21:00 -0700 • Engine revision 425cfb54d0 • Dart version 3.11.4 • DevTools version 2.54.2 • Feature flags: enable-web, enable-linux-desktop, enable-macos-desktop, enable-windows-desktop, enable-android, enable-ios, cli-animations, enable-native-assets, omit-legacy-version-file, enable-lldb-debugging, enable-uiscene-migration [√] Windows Version (11 Pro 64-bit, 25H2, 2009) [2.9s] [√] Android toolchain - develop for Android devices (Android SDK version 35.0.0) [3.1s] • Android SDK at C:\Users\pchar\AppData\Local\Android\Sdk • Emulator version 35.2.10.0 (build_id 12414864) (CL:N/A) • Platform android-36, build-tools 35.0.0 • Java binary at: C:\Program Files\Android\Android Studio\jbr\bin\java This is the JDK bundled with the latest Android Studio installation on this machine. title: TextSelection.isDirectional is not respected, make it do something useful eg: for Mac
body:
It seems like it's not wired up anywhere. We should make it do something useful and test it or remove it.

It's current presence is confusing.
comment_summary: Comments discuss that the behavior `isDirectional` is meant to control is currently handled by `DefaultTextEditingShortcuts`; the value could drive that logic instead of the platform.
comments_raw: We should make it do something, yes. Or at least test that it's set correctly. It's necessary for some platforms (e.g. Mac text selections; not sure about iOS).
There im'ts any example where it is used in the code that i could find, considering this as proposal to make use of `TextSelection.isDirectional`

Currently this logic is handled by our text shortcut code (DefaultTextEditingShortcuts), so this kind of behavior should be correct on all platforms, but yeah I guess this value is not wired up. Maybe we could make the behavior depend on this value instead of just the platform if it's useful? Otherwise remove it if there's no value in wiring that up...

title: Unicode input should be indicated.
body:
Ctrl+Shift+U (on certain operating systems) normally would bring up an underlined "u" in a text field to indicate it's awaiting a Unicode entry. This is not indicated on Flutter, despite it taking the input.

![Flutter](https://i.imgur.com/5LGa38g.png)
![Play Store, expected behavior.](https://i.imgur.com/JDQ2sZt.png)
comment_summary: A commenter confirmed the Ctrl+Shift+U Unicode input indicator is observed on Linux but not on macOS; Windows behavior was assumed to be similar to Linux and the platform label was added accordingly.
comments_raw: Is this something that I should be able to do on a Mac? I don't get anything like that with ctrl- or cmd- shift-u. I will try this on Linux in a bit.
I do see this happen on Linux. Not sure about Windows but I'll assume it does and add the label.

title: TextField doesn't appear within a direction:Axis.vertical Wrap
body: code: ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8746914/62993013-4bea8080-be88-11e9-84ea-93f3908b57bd.png) screenshot:
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug is specific to `direction: Axis.vertical` and does not occur with `Axis.horizontal`. Reproduced on stable and master; the exception is an assertion that `InputDecorator` cannot have an unbounded width, indicating `Wrap` does not provide a bounded width constraint in vertical axis mode. No fix or PR was proposed; issue is stalled.
comments_raw:
Issue still exist when using `direction: Axis.vertical` but no issue when using `direction: Axis.horizontal`

### Code Sample
```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() => runApp(MyApp());

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'Material App',
      home: Scaffold(
        appBar: AppBar(
          title: Text('Material App Bar'),
        ),
        body: Center(
          child: Wrap(
            direction: Axis.vertical,
            children: <Widget>[
              TextField(controller: TextEditingController(text: "123"))
            ],
          ),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

title: Directional navigation key binding defaults should be limited to those platforms that use it.
body:
<!-- Thank you for using Flutter!

     If you are looking for support, please check out our documentation
     or consider asking a question on Stack Overflow:
      * https://flutter.dev/
      * https://api.flutter.dev/
      * https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/flutter?sort=frequent

     If you have found a bug or if our documentation doesn't have an answer
     to what you're looking for, then fill our the template below. Please read
     our guide to filing a bug first: https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports
-->

When running the fluter devtools on macos, I'm seeing strange key handling behavior. There are extra key actions that I don't see on flutter web. Some discussion of the issue, with screenshots, is at https://github.com/flutter/devtools/issues/1769. But essentially:

I'm adding row selection and keyboard navigation to a custom widget TreeTable ([built here](https://github.com/flutter/devtools/blob/ae483b098ec65941f457bf545bdc9059f6537231/packages/devtools_app/lib/src/flutter/table.dart#L363)). I'm explicitly [handling the arrow up/down keys](https://github.com/flutter/devtools/blob/ae483b098ec65941f457bf545bdc9059f6537231/packages/devtools_app/lib/src/flutter/table.dart#L427) to move the selection and scroll the view. But on macos I see scrolling that is not caused by my handler, and also a kind of ghost selected row (see the screenshots in https://github.com/flutter/devtools/issues/1769) not caused by me (the color is different than the one I am using). Also, sometimes after clicking on a row the arrow keys will start cycling through the top-level tabs (Inspector, timeline) instead of the rows in the table.

In a nutshell there is some code handling the up/down arrow keys in a way that I don't understand, can't control, and can't predict. It also does not happen when I run via flutter web which leads me to believe it's a bug in the macos platform code.

The PR that introduced keyboard navigation is here: https://github.com/flutter/devtools/pull/1810

## Steps to Reproduce
$git clone git@github.com:flutter/devtools.git
$cd devtools/packages/devtools_app
$flutter run -d macos

1. Enter the url of another running flutter app
2. Click on the "info" tab
3. Click "performance"
4. Record performance data
5. Click on the "call tree" tab
6. Click inside the call tree table
7. navigate up/down with the arrow keys.
8. Compare with the behavior when running with `-d web`

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->
I expect the arrow up/down keys to cause the window to scroll only when the selection reaches the bottom or top of the viewport. I expect there to be only 1 selected row. I expect that pressing the arrow up/down keys should not escape the treetable and start cycling the top-level tabs of the viewport.

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->
See the examples at https://github.com/flutter/devtools/issues/1769


<details>
  <summary>Logs</summary>

<!--
      Run your application with `flutter run --verbose` and attach all the
      log output below between the lines with the backticks. If there is an
      exception, please see if the error message includes enough information
      to explain how to solve the issue.
-->


```
I captured the logs, but they're really verbose (1600 lines). LMK if there is a better way to provide them besides pasting the whole thing into this issue.
```

<!--
     Run `flutter analyze` and attach any output of that command below.
     If there are any analysis errors, try resolving them before filing this issue.
-->

```

Analyzing devtools_app...

```

comment_summary: Root cause identified as the default directional focus key bindings in `app.dart` being enabled on macOS (and potentially Linux). The fix was to disable them at the top level and re-enable per-widget (e.g., ListView). Linux was noted to also use directional nav in some apps, Windows less so. Workarounds provided: wrap in `Shortcuts` with `Intent.doNothing`, or bind `DirectionalFocusIntent` to `DoNothingAction`. Status: partially addressed; the default bindings were intended to be removed but the issue remained open with stale labels.
comments_raw:
I just noticed that a similar thing happens in the "flutter inspector" tab. This is without any specific key handling for the arrow keys. Focus travels through the inspector tree and other UI elements in an unpredictable way.

In this example I just mouse-click on the root element and then press the up/down/left/right arrow keys to see what happens.

![inspector-arrow-keys](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3227/79902998-363e3b00-83c7-11ea-815d-2a7d90db21c2.gif)
cc @stuartmorgan

I noticed that I can get similar behavior from the flutter gallery app.

In macos, the arrow keys move through the buttons:
![gallery-arrow-keys](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3227/79906160-2d039d00-83cc-11ea-8b0e-4968da65c1f7.gif)

Similar to what "tab" does in flutter web; but while "tab" cycles through the UI elements, the macos arrow keys are "smart": left/right moves strictly back and forth and up/down moves focus vertically (example: with the "info" button highlighted, pressing down will focus "sign in").

In simple cases I could see this as desirable behavior, but with the kind of custom UI we have in the devtools it definitely is not. Maybe I just need to know how to turn it off?

> Maybe I just need to know how to turn it off?

I believe you can turn it off per https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/45102, but absent a strong argument in favor of having them, I think the right fix here is to have these not be part of the default set on desktop.

You're right, I think these are probably not correct for macOS. I'll disable them.

I did look into the user interface guidelines for keyboard traversal on macOS at the time I defined those, but I didn't find guidance that was all that helpful there one way or another.

When you disable them for macOS, maybe refactor a bit to have _defaultDesktopShortcuts, so that we don't have the same bug crop up for Linux and Windows? We can always re-specialize them later if we find places we want to diverge.
Linux (Debian) at least (I can't test windows at the moment) does do this kind of navigation, at least in their system settings app and a couple of others I tested.
I'm seeing some control-specific things on Windows (e.g., if I'm in a list, or in a menu, then it navigates within that context, as on macOS), but nothing that jumps to other control areas.

Actually, just disabling it won't work.  I'm going to need to disable it at the top level, and then add it back in for each of the things that should be able to navigate in that way (e.g. for a list view, you should be able to navigate in the scroll axis).

@gspencergoog Ah. This only partially helps me, since both my widgets are rendered in ListViews.  Is there a way to control key navigation at that level? https://github.com/flutter/devtools/blob/6d9cd23f61c3bd98a32d84acbce0d25968b49021/packages/devtools_app/lib/src/flutter/table.dart#L661

You can always just turn it off, or bind it to another action.

To turn it off, you can put a `Shortcuts` widget around your view with those keys bound to "Intent.doNothing".  Or bind them to your intent (and action) if you want to do something different.

Or, if you want to turn off directional navigation, but don't want to know the keys it is bound to, you can also bind a `DirectionalFocusIntent` to `DoNothingAction()` in an `Actions` widget around your view.
Is this still valid?
Yes, this is still valid.
The `triaged-desktop` label is irrelevant if there is no `team-desktop` label or `fyi-desktop` label.

title: Let RenderEditable use LineMetrics instead of assuming every line has the same height
body:
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/02d441ea55b328133c266991f43b0a1148edb63f/packages/flutter/lib/src/rendering/editable.dart#L864-L873

Vertical key movements may skip a line if that line is way shorter than its adjacent lines.
comment_summary: A commenter added a concrete example illustrating the expected caret behavior for up/down arrow key navigation across lines of varying length, where the caret should maintain its horizontal offset across lines rather than snapping to line end.
comments_raw:
Additionally in a left-aligned text field:
```
aaaaaaa|
aaa
aaaaaaaaaa
```
pressing <kbd>↓</kbd> once should move the caret to the end of the second line:
```
aaaaaaa
aaa|
aaaaaaaaaa
```
press <kbd>↓</kbd> again should move the caret to the 3rd line, but further to the right:
```
aaaaaaa
aaa
aaaaaaa|aaa
```

title: Document "ghost run" and its interaction with `Paragraph.getBoxesForRange`
body:
Current the only documentation I could find is a code comment: https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/cc9f859004f88a84f6d42e234c8be0ecaf66c46f/third_party/txt/src/txt/paragraph_txt.cc#L742-L752

>       // A "ghost" run is a run that does not impact the layout, breaking,
>      // alignment, width, etc but is still "visible" through getRectsForRange.
>      // For example, trailing whitespace on centered text can be scrolled
>      // through with the caret but will not wrap the line.
>      //
>      // Here, we add an additional run for the whitespace, but dont
>      // let it impact metrics. After layout of the whitespace run, we do not
>      // add its width into the x-offset adjustment, effectively nullifying its
>     // impact on the layout.

which implies `getRectsForRange` can return text boxes that are outside of the paragraph's bounding box if called on a ghost run. It's likely a common assumption that text boxes is always within the paragraph's bounding box so that should be documented somewhere.

title: [MacOS] Trailing whitespace in multiline TextField overflows and doesn't go to newline.
body:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Use a multiline TextField.
2. Input a lot of whitespace in the content tail.
3. Whitespace is overflow and not switch to a newline (Tap Ctrl + A to select all).

**Expected results:** Auto switch to a new line.

**Actual results:** Whitespace is overflow.

<details>

```dart
const SizedBox(
    width: 400,
    height: 200,
    child: TextField(
      maxLines: null,
      keyboardType: TextInputType.multiline,
      decoration: InputDecoration(
        border: InputBorder.none,
        filled: true,
        fillColor: Colors.pink

      expands: true,
```

comment_summary: Comments confirm this is distinct from mobile behavior: on macOS/desktop, trailing whitespace in a multiline TextField does not trigger line wrap, unlike Sublime Text or other native editors. On iOS/mobile, whitespace also does not wrap, but subsequent characters start on a new line. Selection extending horizontally outside the field when selecting text with trailing whitespace is noted as a separate bug. Attributed to Skia-level text layout behavior. No fix; stalled.

title: canvas.drawLine() does not paint correctly on Samsung Galaxy Note 20 Ultra
body:
## Steps to Reproduce 1. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample using Samsung Galaxy S4 (GT-I9500) **Expected results:** [Dartpad code](https://dartpad.dev/?id=a02554870741d78d26f0669b808ae5ab) **Actual results:**
Code sample ``` import 'package:flutter/material.dart'; void main() { runApp(MyApp()); } class MyApp extends StatelessWidget { const MyApp({Key key}) : super(key: key); @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { return MaterialApp(debugShowCheckedModeBanner: false, home: HomeScreen(), ); } } class HomeScreen extends StatelessWidget { const HomeScreen({Key key}) : super(key: key); @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { return Scaffold( appBar: AppBar(title: Text('UnderlineInputBorder'),centerTitle: true,), body: Container( margin: EdgeInsets.all(20), child: TextField( decoration: InputDecoration(enabledBorder: UnderlineInputBorder()), ), ), ); } } ```
Flutter Doctor ``` [√] Flutter (Channel stable, 3.0.5, on Microsoft Windows [Version 10.0.19044.1889], locale en-US) • Flutter version 3.0.5 at C:\src\flutter • Upstream repository https://github.com/flutter/flutter.git • Framework revision f1875d570e (7 weeks ago), 2022-07-13 11:24:16 -0700 • Engine revision e85ea0e79c • Dart version 2.17.6 • DevTools version 2.12.2 [√] Android toolchain - develop for Android devices (Android SDK version 33.0.0) • Android SDK at C:\Users\****\AppData\Local\Android\Sdk • Platform android-33, build-tools 33.0.0 • Java binary at: C:\Program Files\Android\Android Studio1\jre\bin\java • Java version OpenJDK Runtime Environment (build 11.0.13+0-b1751.21-8125866) • All Android licenses accepted. Doctor found issues in 1 category. ```
Other devices are painting as expected, however I got painting issue on this device (GT-I9500). A year ago I faced similar painting issue discussed here: [BoxDecoration has bug when using both Border and BorderRadius](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/86794) I tried to change **UnderlineInputBorder** to **OutlineInputBorder** and got expected results without any issues, because OutlineInputBorder uses `canvas.drawPath()` to draw it. On the other side, UnderlineInputBorder uses `canvas.drawLine() ` which is causing this issue. If I am not wrong **drawLine** calls native code called **Canvas_drawLine**

title: Provide an API to detect if a TextPosition is located at a soft word wrap
body:
Currently it is not possible to detect if a TextPosition is located at a soft word wrap. We should provide an API, most likely through `RenderEditable` that gives this information. This is necessary to accomplish the use case below.

## Use case
On iOS we want to be able to toggle the toolbar if we tap on the same `TextPosition` regardless of the `TextAffinity` when our tap is not at a soft word wrap. When our tap is at a soft word wrap we want to hide the toolbar if the `TextAffinity` has changed and toggle the toolbar if it hasn't.

## Proposal
Provide an API through `RenderEditable` that tells us if a given `TextPosition` is located at a word wrap.

title: Extending to paragraph/or word boundary on MacOS should default to the `downstream` position when at a word wrap
body:
When using keyboard shortcuts on MacOS
* shift + option + up/down to extend to the next/previous paragraph boundary
* shift + option + left/right to extend to the next/previous word boundary

And the caret is positioned at a word wrap initially, and the selection is extended, when returning to the original position (collapsing at the base when the selection inverts) the resulting selection should to default to the `downstream`  position.

Current Behavior:
The selection inherits the `affinity` from `selection.base.affinity`.

Expected Behavior:
The selection defaults to a `downstream` affinity.

Expected|Actual
---|---
<video src="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/948037/215957886-fbafa1e7-8b3a-4df4-8403-f88dc84e372d.mov" />|<video src="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/948037/215958318-7da3618b-2362-4791-97f9-7c3c19e97b33.mov" />

title: [Windows] Incorrect character deletion in right-to-left texts
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

Type rtl text to multiline textfield :
(DO NOT COPY AND PASTE)

بابا آب داد
با مهربانی
4طتانا جمعا 4واتا +یکسوییت 20متری
بنبابا 40ساله

### Expected results

The letter to the right of the cursor should be deleted.
(It should be noted that when I copy the text from above and paste it in the text field, everything works correctly.)

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/32570964/b3d09305-3b20-4388-b938-2ce451217a79



### Actual results

But when I type the rtl text , the letter on the left side of the cursor is deleted.

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/32570964/31f9ee2d-3aeb-441a-8568-97dcf7fdefe7



### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:flutter_localizations/flutter_localizations.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({Key? key}) : super(key: key);

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'Flutter Demo',
      locale: const Locale("ar"),
      localizationsDelegates: const [
        GlobalMaterialLocalizations.delegate,
        GlobalWidgetsLocalizations.delegate,
        GlobalCupertinoLocalizations.delegate,
      ],
      supportedLocales: const [Locale("ar")],
      theme: ThemeData(
        primarySwatch: Colors.blue,
      ),
      home: const MyHomePage(title: 'Flutter Demo Home Page'),
    );
  }
}

class MyHomePage extends StatefulWidget {
  const MyHomePage({Key? key, required this.title}) : super(key: key);

  final String title;

  @override
  State<MyHomePage> createState() => _MyHomePageState();
}

class _MyHomePageState extends State<MyHomePage> {
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      appBar: AppBar(
        title: Text(widget.title),
      ),
      body: const Center(
        child: Padding(
          padding: EdgeInsets.all(16),
          child: TextField(
            maxLines: 1000,
          ),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}

```

</details>


### Screenshots or Video

<details>
<summary>Screenshots / Video demonstration</summary>

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/32570964/d06fc103-aca5-4df4-8242-fdc400247346

</details>


### Logs

<details><summary>Logs</summary>

```console
[Paste your logs here]
```

</details>

comment_summary: Comments confirmed reproduction on Windows with RTL (Persian/Farsi) multiline text that was manually typed (not pasted); pasting the same text works correctly. The root cause was identified as a caret placement issue: when text contains CRLF-style line endings from manual typing, the cursor offset reported by the framework is incorrect for RTL text in multiline mode on Windows. A commenter linked this to issue #140739 and suspected a connection to CRLF handling. Another commenter noted the bug also manifests with custom fonts on Android (wrong caret position and incorrect word selection). A user shared a workaround that manually inserts `\r\n` instead of `\n` on Enter. The assignee (LongCatIsLooong) was removed after no status updates.

I tried  `20متری`  in an RTL paragraph but couldn't reproduce the issue. @mohammadjalalis could you provide more detailed instructions to help us reproduce this?

In single-line mode, everything is OK.
But in multi-line mode, when we decide to delete a letter, usually the next letter is deleted. And in general, the placement of the cursor in multi-line mode in right-to-left texts is very problematic.

Video description:

From second 1 to 25, you can see that everything is correct in single-line mode, and when we try to remove the letter "ر", the same "ر" is removed.

From 25 to 1:40, we type a three-line RTL text(Persian language), when we decide to remove the letter "ر" in the second line, the letter "ی" is removed instead, which is incorrect.

From 1:40 to the end with the arrow keys on the keyboard, when we move the pointer over the text, the cursor is not in the right place, and sometimes it moves three times on some letters. For example, in the first line, the cursor moves three times on the word "آب" and the letter "ب".
But the interesting thing is that in the last line, the cursor moves completely correct and is placed at the end of each letter and spaces.


https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/32570964/12e18d3a-a5a0-42b2-8080-e566a11a4e5c

Tip:
I recently noticed an issue, that is, when we type the same text, for example, in the GitHub editor and copy it to the Flutter Textfield, everything is exactly as we expect.
The reason is the addition of "\r" before "\n". 
I hope this tip helps.
In this video, I copied and pasted the following text into the Textfield.

بابا آب داد
20 متری
تا ابد

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/32570964/6e563a8e-4fcc-4f6f-9d1c-1e45ee6cdfce
![image_2024-01-01_13-25-52 copy](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/32570964/2ce71965-ff99-444c-b011-78c7c3abca87)

Thanks for the detailed repro! When the caret offset is 19 the caret should be placed after "ی" 

![image](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/31859944/bb15537b-44d4-4d4a-b402-b3390b285fbb)

So this appears to be a caret placement issue.

Ah I reproduced this on Windows. 
![image](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/31859944/caccd4c7-5aad-4fb1-8973-c2f584292d27)

It's interesting that this is platform specific since the cursor placement logic in the framework is supposed to be platform-agnostic. I doubt this has to do with DirectWrite. Maybe this has something to do with CRLF.

Maybe related: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/140739

When we use the built-in font on Android, everything works fine; however, there are issues when we use a custom font.
In this case, the caret is not positioned correctly and the selection of words is incorrect.

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/32570964/166ef398-6999-4b61-9eaa-fe8c92583eaa
@LongCatIsLooong Did you check this out?

This is important and has a huge impact on the performance of the RTL applications. Please check and fix this issue.
@LongCatIsLooong @Renzo-Olivares @darshankawar

This issue is assigned to @LongCatIsLooong but has had no recent status updates. Please consider unassigning this issue if it is not going to be addressed in the near future. This allows people to have a clearer picture of what work is actually planned. Thanks!
This issue was assigned to @LongCatIsLooong but has had no status updates in a long time. To remove any ambiguity about whether the issue is being worked on, the assignee was removed.

title: Arrow key navigation gets stuck at the end of RTL text using Samsung Keyboard
body:
```dart
const text = 'ی';
final controller = TextEditingController(text: text * 10)..selection = TextSelection.collapsed(offset: 10); // Selection is placed at the logical end of the text.

TextField(textDirection: TextDirection.rtl, controller: controller, autofocus: true),
```
## Steps to reproduce
1. wait for the text field autofocus
2. switch to the Samsung keyboard
3. press <kbd>←</kbd> to move the cursor to the start of the text (since flutter currently uses logical order for arrow key navigation).

## Expected behavior:
the cursor moves to the right

## Actual behavior:
the cursor does not move.
comment_summary: Root cause identified: Samsung keyboard ignores the left-arrow key when the cursor is already at the visual left end of RTL text, because Flutter uses logical order for arrow key navigation while Samsung keyboard uses visual order. Gboard is not affected. On native, arrow key navigation moves in visual order which Flutter does not currently support; a commenter notes this is a long-standing architectural gap.
comments_raw:
This does not happen on Gboard. It seems that the Samsung keyboard ignores the <kbd>←</kbd> key when the cursor is already at the left end of the text. 

## Screen recording

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/31859944/3aac9554-a946-4c1e-a376-7be13bc75246

I can also reproduce this with Samsung keyboard.

<details>
<summary>Complete sample code</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(
    const MaterialApp(
      title: 'Flutter Demo',
      home: Home(),
    ),
  );
}

class Home extends StatelessWidget {
  const Home({
    super.key,
  });

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    const text = 'ی';
    final controller = TextEditingController(text: text * 10)
      ..selection = const TextSelection.collapsed(offset: 10); // Selection is placed at the logical end of the text.
    return Scaffold(
      body: Center(
        child: TextField(
          textDirection: TextDirection.rtl,
          controller: controller,
          autofocus: true,
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}

```
</details>

title: Error where possible null is being asserted in rendering paragraph
body:
### Steps to reproduce

Spam asynchronous redraws of a Text element on macOS by resizing the window

### Expected results

No Error

### Actual results

